# Figures and captions

**The block.** A `@figure` holds a graphic plus a caption and a `label`. The graphic can be an uploaded image or a vector chart (pgfplots/TikZ) generated by the chart tool.

**Example.**
```lml
@figure[label="fig:arch"]
  \includegraphics[width=0.8\linewidth]{architecture.pdf}
  @caption
    The model architecture.
```
Refer to it with `Figure~(fig:arch)`; Lilia numbers and places it.

**Vector vs raster.** Prefer vector (PDF/pgfplots) so figures stay crisp and match the paper's fonts. The **chart** tool emits a ready `@figure` with a pgfplots plot from your data.

**Tips.** Size with `\linewidth` fractions, not fixed `cm`, so figures adapt to the column width. Every figure you discuss should have a `fig:` label. Related: latex-chart, cross-references, the `lilia-figure` skill.
